_doc: JStack systems registry — every system bundled by the JStack plugin (hooks, skills, runtime mechanisms) that must be tracked, documented, and live-testable. The host registry (e.g. ~/Operations/Infrastructure/config/systems.json on the Jarvis machine) federates this file via its `imports:` field and stamps each entry with `origin: "jstack"` + the resolved plugin version at read time. Maintenance rule: every entry lives where its code lives — edit JStack systems in JStack, host systems in the host registry, never both. Every entry MUST declare a `test` so verification is callable on demand from any surface (CLI, dashboard, work-Claude debugging).

_schema.kind: hook | skill | system | subsystem — 'hook' = Claude Code lifecycle hook (PreToolUse, SessionStart, etc.). 'skill' = invocable slash-command skill shipped under skills/. 'system' = composite mechanism (rules-injection pipeline, install flow). 'subsystem' = nested sub-feature inside a parent.
_schema.name: string — display name
_schema.code: array of code paths (relative to plugin root)
_schema.config: array of config paths (relative to plugin root unless ~/-prefixed)
_schema.docs: array of doc paths inside the plugin (typically docs/systems/{id}.md)

test.type: script | endpoint — 'script' = CLI executable, runs in-place, exit 0 = pass. 'endpoint' = HTTP POST handled by a host process (reserved for host-side wrappers).
test.path: string — for type=script, path relative to plugin root. for type=endpoint, URL path.
test.description: string — one-line description of what the test verifies

systems.name: Cross-Tree Rule Injection
systems.description: PreToolUse hook that injects path-matched ~/.claude/rules/*.md bodies into Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit calls — works regardless of session launch CWD, so cross-tree agents pick up the right conventions on every file mutation.

test.description: Live test of the real hook script via its PreToolUse JSON stdin contract: cold-fire (matches rule + emits additionalContext on first call), consecutive-dedup (same session, no transcript growth → no re-inject), threshold re-fire (transcript grown past JSTACK_RULE_REINJECT_BYTES → re-inject). Runs hermetically against a temp fixture via JSTACK_RULES_DIR + JSTACK_CACHE_ROOT overrides — never touches real markers or shipped rules.

systems.notes: Registered in hooks/hooks.json — Claude Code auto-loads when the plugin is installed. Kill switch: JSTACK_PATH_RULES_DISABLED=1. Re-fire threshold: JSTACK_RULE_REINJECT_BYTES (default 400000 ≈ 100K tokens). Defensive: any exception → silent exit 0, never blocks the tool. Marker files live at /tmp/jstack-rule-cache/<session_id>/<rule>.marker storing the transcript byte offset at last injection.

systems.name: SessionStart Continuity Injection
systems.description: SessionStart hook that injects the sub-mode's continuity.md (running memory) into every new session as additionalContext — the READ half of the post-session-review loop (the WRITE half is the review's Phase D). continuity.md is not context just by existing on disk; this makes a session start sighted instead of cold. state.md is NOT injected (it's the active-items index, read as a file). Sub-mode resolves from cwd: first segment under {agent_root}/{Name}, else 'chat' (cockpit sessions stay at the agent root, so the project-dir key / memory is never moved); 'review' is skipped.

test.description: Live test of the real hook via its SessionStart JSON stdin contract against a hermetic temp agents tree (JSTACK_REVIEW_CONFIG override): agent-root cwd → 'chat' injects chat continuity and NOT state.md; explicit submode cwd → that submode's continuity; 'review' submode → no output; non-workspace cwd → no-op; agent without review/ → not recognized; reviewable agent with state but no continuity → no output; emitted payload is valid SessionStart hook JSON.

systems.notes: Registered in hooks/hooks.json — auto-loads when the plugin is installed. Kill switch: JSTACK_CONTINUITY_INJECT_DISABLED=1. Reads agent_root from JSTACK_REVIEW_CONFIG (default ~/.claude/jstack/review.json), same as the engine. Defensive: any error → exit 0, never blocks a session. Sub-mode resolution is kept identical to bin/session-review-spawn resolve_submode and the skill's Phase D writer — if they drift, the next run won't find what the review wrote.

systems.name: Install Rules
systems.description: Symlinks (or copies, with --copy) every rule under rules-stage/ into ~/.claude/rules/ so the rule bodies auto-load by glob match on every Claude Code session.

test.description: Validates SKILL.md exists, frontmatter is well-formed, name matches directory.

systems.notes: Default mode symlinks (auto-track plugin updates); --copy snapshots for offline edit; --force overwrites existing files. Symlinks point into the plugin cache, so a version bump invalidates the link target — re-run with --force after major updates. /jstack:install-rules.

systems.name: Work
systems.description: Gets the agent battle-ready on a topic before doing the work: orient (date + own state.md), resolve project → repo, determine stack, load every relevant skill via the Skill tool, survey recent changes (git log/status + topic file search), read the core files, and report a grounded lay of the land. Preparation only — no code changes, no state writes, no PR.

test.description: Validates SKILL.md exists, frontmatter is well-formed, name matches directory.

systems.notes: Args: [@project] <topic>. No registry read — leans on the agent's own loaded identity for project + stack. @project resolves a repo name case-insensitively under userConfig.repo_root (default parent of agent_root); unknown → list candidates and stop. No @project → own project; multi-project agent + ambiguous topic → infer from topic, else ask one question. Skill selection matches stack+topic against live skill descriptions (no hardcoded table), loads inclusive. /jstack:work.

systems.name: Handoff
systems.description: Generates a concise session handoff doc and opens a fresh Claude Code terminal preloaded with it via bin/open-terminal-here. Supports @agent targeting: resolves another agent's workspace under user_config.agent_root and boots the new session there — walk-up does the identity switch.

test.description: Validates SKILL.md exists, frontmatter is well-formed, bin/open-terminal-here adapter is executable.

systems.notes: Args: [@agent] [focus]. @agent resolves {Name} case-insensitively under agent_root; target cwd = chat/ subdir if present else agent root; unknown name → list available agents and stop. Focus narrows scope. Writes handoff-context.md to the TARGET cwd, then open-terminal-here with --append-system-prompt-file. Cross-agent docs are briefings: name who hands off, mark user-locked decisions. Adapter overridable earlier in PATH. /jstack:handoff.

systems.name: Audit
systems.description: Writes a claims document (audit-brief.md: protocol + Original Issue / What Was Done / Claimed Verifications / Caution Flags / Blast Radius / Pitfalls) and opens a fresh terminal via bin/open-terminal-here where a trust-nothing auditor verifies every claim from source and reports CONFIRMED/REFUTED/UNVERIFIABLE. Report-only — the auditor changes nothing.

test.description: Validates SKILL.md exists, frontmatter is well-formed, bin/open-terminal-here adapter is executable.

systems.notes: Args: [focus] [@agent], order-agnostic. @agent resolves like handoff under agent_root (auditor boots under that agent's identity). Caution Flags section is mandatory — user-stated 'don't break X' constraints verbatim, verified first. Auto-start: kickoff prompt passed as a pre-quoted positional claude arg (adapter flattens args via $*). Auditor asks first only if genuinely blocked, else begins immediately. /jstack:audit.

systems.name: Save
systems.description: Files the current conversation as an active item under {agent_root}/{Name}/active/{slug}.md with structured progress + reference + a follow-up filed via bin/file-followup.

test.description: Validates SKILL.md exists, frontmatter is well-formed, bin/file-followup adapter is executable.

systems.notes: Resolves the running agent from cwd via the userConfig.agent_root. Enforces convention max 3 active items per agent. Mirrors a one-line entry to state.md if present. Follow-up backend (none/todo/reminders/slack) routed by userConfig.followup_backend + userConfig.followup_target. /jstack:save.

systems.name: Active
systems.description: Lists or loads active items for the running agent — sorted by filed date, numbered stably (1 = oldest), max 3 per convention.

test.description: Validates SKILL.md exists, frontmatter is well-formed, name matches directory.

systems.notes: List mode (no arg): one line per active item. Load mode (n | last | oldest): brief on goal, where-I-am, next moves, reference. Post-brief work mutates the active doc in-place: bumps last_touched, appends progress log entry, revises next-moves checkboxes. /jstack:active.

systems.name: Timeline Log
systems.description: Daily timeline files ({timeline_dir}/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md) written by bin/log_event — block format, chronological insertion, pipeline-task consolidation. The day's spine: feeds daily briefs and nightly reviews.

test.description: Runs the real bin/log_event against a temp JSTACK_TIMELINE_DIR: block format, detail bullets, chronological insertion, blank-line separation, pipeline-task consolidation (one live block, earliest ts), headline collapse, bad-arg rejection.

systems.notes: Self-contained python3 stdlib — no venv, no host imports. Timeline dir override: JSTACK_TIMELINE_DIR (default ~/Logs/Timeline). Format spec is the rules-stage/timeline.md rule (install via /jstack:install-rules). Hosts with an in-process timeline writer must keep format parity — pin it with a host-side parity test against this bin.

systems.name: Post-Session Review
systems.description: SessionEnd hook → bin/session-review-spawn engine → claude --print from {agent_root}/{Name}/review/ running the review skill → output parser-validated (required sections, evidence floors, timeline-grew check) → retry → escalate. Keeps state.md, active items, and the timeline truthful after every session.

test.description: Imports the real engine under a hermetic temp config: output validator (good passes; missing section / evidence-free walk / log_event-claimed-but-timeline-didn't-grow reject; 'no user turns' + cron paraphrases accepted; host-extended section lists enforced), agent resolution (umbrella dirs, project_dir_map, $HOME → default_agent, review/-dir reviewable convention), atomic claim dedup (live pid loses, stale pid taken over), SPAWN log-line dashboard contract.

systems.notes: Stack requirements: agent workspaces under agent_root with {Name}/review/ (reviewable convention), the review skill installed (default /jstack:post-session-review; hosts may point skill_invocation at their own playbook), bin/log_event on the spawned PATH (engine prepends its own bin/). Config: JSTACK_REVIEW_CONFIG (default ~/.claude/jstack/review.json) — agent_root, default_agent, project_dir_map, required_sections, model, timeouts, slots, tg_classify_cmd, escalate_cmd, extra_path, log_file. Kill switch: JSTACK_REVIEW_DISABLED=1. Loop guard: SKIP_SESSION_HOOK=1 on every spawn. Multi-entry-point safe: per-session atomic claim means a host SessionEnd hook and the plugin hook never double-review.

systems.name: Push
systems.description: Commits and pushes the work touched in the current session to whatever repo(s) those files live in — supports session-scoped (default) or all-pending (`all`) modes, and `0` to commit without pushing.

test.description: Validates SKILL.md exists, frontmatter is well-formed, name matches directory.

systems.notes: Authorization gate: only runs if the user explicitly said push/commit/ship/land in this session. Resolves repos from the touched-file list (works from a non-repo cockpit). Hard rules: no --no-verify, no --amend, no Co-Authored-By, no `git add -A`, sensitive paths skipped, never force-push main/master. `all` mode groups pending changes into per-unit-of-work commits with a one-line plan up front. /jstack:push.

systems.name: Show Me
systems.description: Surfaces the result of the current topic in its real viewer instead of describing it. A fidelity-ladder command: mode token raw|live picks source-code vs live-running surface, default prefers an in-code preview. Classifies the artifact (code, preview, image, doc, mockup, data, app, web page, diff) and opens it via bin/open-artifact / Xcode (xed) / sim. Optional focus arg narrows which artifact.

test.description: Validates SKILL.md exists, frontmatter is well-formed, bin/open-artifact adapter is executable.

systems.notes: Args: [raw|live] [focus]. mode = reserved token on the fidelity ladder: raw -> source code (iOS: xed <View>.swift in Xcode; mockup/doc -> its .html/.svg/.md source not the render); default -> cheapest visual, prefers an in-code preview (SwiftUI #Preview in Xcode canvas, Storybook, Quick Look) over a heavier surface; live -> running env (sim build+launch+screenshot, dev server + live URL) via the project's run/sim/device skill. focus narrows the artifact; omitted shows the most recent salient result. Static artifacts (image/pdf): default==live==open in viewer, raw==source-if-any. Fallbacks: requested surface unavailable -> drop to nearest rung and say so; no visual -> textual render; adapter/xed nonzero -> report absolute path. bin/open-artifact self-detects platform (open / xdg-open|gio / start), overridable earlier in PATH. /jstack:showme.

systems.name: Day Audit
systems.description: Reverifies a whole day's shipped work across every repo, each audited under its OWNING agent for real domain context. Treats that day's timeline (the agents' claims) as a claims document, gathers the day's commits in each repo under repo_root (all refs), resolves each repo to its owning agent via the agent registry, fans out one regression auditor per repo-with-commits told to load that agent's role file + the repo's CLAUDE.md first (focus on bug-fix commits: real fix? regressed another issue? broke a consumer? smuggled in something big?), then synthesizes an IMPROVED/NEUTRAL/REGRESSED verdict per app and flags claim<->commit gaps. Report-only on code; always writes one timeline block recording the verdict.

test.description: Validates SKILL.md exists, frontmatter is well-formed, name matches directory, bin/log_event is executable.

systems.notes: Args: [today|yesterday|<date>], date normalized in python3 (defaults today). Portable repo discovery: repo_root userConfig, else parent of agent_root, scanned for */.git. Ownership: agent_registry userConfig (else {agent_root}/agents.json) — each agent entry's {workspace, repos}; repo<->registry matched by normalized name (case + _/- folded) against BOTH local dir basename and git origin, so WBIS_iOS==WBIS-iOS. Owning-agent context delivered by instructing the auditor to read {workspace}/CLAUDE.md + {repo}/CLAUDE.md first (in-process subagents don't inherit a foreign CLAUDE.md via cwd); unowned repo -> generic auditor, coverage never drops. Per-day commits via `git log --all --no-merges --since/--until --date=local` so develop/feature/pushed work is covered. Verdict logged to the AUDITED day's file: `log_event day-audit --at <now> --date <DAY> --pipeline-task day-audit#<DAY>` — one block per day, replaced on re-run. Report-only on repos (never edits code); high-severity findings re-verified by the parent before repeating. /jstack:day-audit.
